# Copilot Instructions for International Rishta

- Stack: Next.js 15 App Router + TypeScript + Tailwind; client-heavy marketing flows with Framer Motion. I18n is required everywhere via `next-intl`.
- Routing: All pages live under the locale segment; middleware forces locale prefixes (`/en`, `/ur`). New routes must be nested under `[locale]` and link with `useLocale`/`Link` paths (e.g., `/${locale}/pricing`). See [src/middleware.ts](src/middleware.ts) and [src/app/[locale]/page.tsx](src/app/%5Blocale%5D/page.tsx).
- I18n patterns: Layout loads messages and sets `dir`/fonts from locale in [src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx](src/app/%5Blocale%5D/layout.tsx). Add message keys to both locale files under [src/locales/en/common.json](src/locales/en/common.json) and [src/locales/ur/common.json](src/locales/ur/common.json); access via `useTranslations` (see [src/components/Navigation.tsx](src/components/Navigation.tsx)).
- RTL readiness: Tailwind uses `tailwindcss-logical`; prefer logical classes (`ps-*`, `pe-*`, `text-start/end`) and rely on `dir` from layout. Brand fonts are wired via CSS vars; pick `font-nastaliq` for Urdu, `font-poppins` otherwise.
- Styling tokens: Use custom colors `gold` and `teal`, radius `rounded-card`/`rounded-pill` from [tailwind.config.ts](tailwind.config.ts). Keep hero/video gradients and glass cards consistent with existing sections like [src/components/VideoHero.tsx](src/components/VideoHero.tsx) and [src/components/PricingPreview.tsx](src/components/PricingPreview.tsx).
- Navigation & locale switch: Header animates and swaps locale links; update nav items in one place ([src/components/Navigation.tsx](src/components/Navigation.tsx)). Subscription counter currently mocked; avoid hard-coding counts in new spots.
- Pages: Home composes hero/how-it-works/trust/pricing blocks. Discover, Pricing, Bureau pages use client components with motion and shared Navigation/Footer; mirror their layout and gradient hero patterns before adding new sections (see [src/app/[locale]/discover/page.tsx](src/app/%5Blocale%5D/discover/page.tsx), [src/app/[locale]/pricing/page.tsx](src/app/%5Blocale%5D/pricing/page.tsx), [src/app/[locale]/bureau/page.tsx](src/app/%5Blocale%5D/bureau/page.tsx)).
- Auth flows: Signup/signin are multi-step UI stubs; hook Supabase later via helpers, not ad-hoc clients. Keep stateful steps client-side and reuse CTA/link structure; see [src/app/[locale]/auth/signup/page.tsx](src/app/%5Blocale%5D/auth/signup/page.tsx).
- Supabase client usage: use wrappers in [src/lib/supabase/client.ts](src/lib/supabase/client.ts) (browser) and [src/lib/supabase/server.ts](src/lib/supabase/server.ts) (server) instead of creating new clients. Env vars expected: `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`; service role stays server-only. Schema source lives in [supabase/schema.sql](supabase/schema.sql); setup steps in [supabase/README.md](supabase/README.md).
- Utilities: Prefer `cn` from [src/lib/utils.ts](src/lib/utils.ts) for Tailwind class merging.
- Assets: Brand/logo/videos reside under `public/assets` and `assets/`; existing hero uses `/assets/Banner-Video-*.mp4` and poster `Banner - International RishtaConnecting Hearts Worldwide.png`. Reuse these before adding new media.
- Motion/animation: Components rely on Framer Motion; wrap animated sections with sensible initial/animate props and `viewport` options like existing cards.
- Build/lint: `npm run dev`, `npm run build`, `npm run start -p 3000`, `npm run lint`. No custom test suite defined.
- Hosting note: README includes cPanel deployment and env hints; keep start script `next start -p 3000` intact.
- Content strategy: The root README is a requirements blueprint (MVP scope, pricing, data model). Treat it as source of truth for copy/features; keep pricing (PKR 3,999 referral / 4,999 direct) and city/license constraints consistent in new UI.
- Internationalization UX: when adding links/buttons, ensure locale-aware hrefs and translated labels; avoid hard-coded English text—add keys under `common.*`.
- Accessibility/perf: Video hero autoplays muted/loop with gradient overlay; maintain text contrast and provide poster fallback. Respect `prefers-reduced-motion` if adding heavier motion.
